project.name: Module 16d: Panic Button
project.description: Pro-gated panic button (Alt+P keyboard shortcut). Closes all browser tabs instantly, saves their URLs as bookmarks in a 'Panic Save [timestamp]' folder for later recovery. Optional browsing history clearing. Designed for the embarrassment-factor user segment. Stays client-side forever — no SyncableFeature needed (pure UI action). Gated by useProGate().
project.parent_plan: Hush Consolidated Build Plan — Day 5 (Pro features)

user_stories.title: lib/panic.ts — tab collection and panic save logic
user_stories.description: Create lib/panic.ts with: collectOpenTabs() → gets all tabs from all windows via browser.tabs.query. buildPanicFolder(tabs, timestamp) → creates a Folder (from lib/types.ts) containing a Bookmark for each open tab. clearBrowsingHistory() → clears all browsing history via browser.history.deleteAll(), guarded by runtime permission check. Pure lib/ module, zero React imports. Uses browser.* APIs via dependency injection for testability.
steps: RED: Test: 'collectOpenTabs returns all non-extension tabs'
RED: Test: 'collectOpenTabs filters out chrome:// and extension:// URLs'
RED: Test: 'buildPanicFolder creates Folder with timestamp in name'
RED: Test: 'buildPanicFolder creates Bookmark child for each tab'
RED: Test: 'buildPanicFolder handles tabs with no title (uses URL as title)'
RED: Test: 'buildPanicFolder returns Folder and Bookmark types from lib/types.ts'
RED: Test: 'clearBrowsingHistory calls history.deleteAll()'
RED: Test: 'clearBrowsingHistory checks permissions.contains before calling API'
RED: Test: 'clearBrowsingHistory returns false when history permission not granted'

GREEN: Create lib/panic.ts. collectOpenTabs: query all tabs, filter to http/https URLs only. buildPanicFolder: returns Folder node (lib/types.ts) with name 'Panic Save YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss', children are Bookmark nodes. clearBrowsingHistory: checks browser.permissions.contains({ permissions: ['history'] }) first, calls browser.history.deleteAll() if granted, returns boolean success.
REFACTOR: Ensure lib/ purity. Dependency injection for browser.tabs, browser.history, browser.permissions.
acceptance_criteria: collectOpenTabs: returns Tab[] filtered to http/https URLs only
buildPanicFolder: returns Folder (from lib/types.ts) with name 'Panic Save YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss'
buildPanicFolder: each tab → Bookmark (from lib/types.ts) with { type: 'bookmark', id, title, url, dateAdded }
buildPanicFolder: handles undefined tab.title gracefully (uses URL as fallback)
clearBrowsingHistory: checks browser.permissions.contains({ permissions: ['history'] }) before API call
clearBrowsingHistory: calls browser.history.deleteAll() only when permission is granted
clearBrowsingHistory: returns false (no throw) when permission not granted
Uses existing Bookmark and Folder types from lib/types.ts — no new type definitions
All functions pure or DI-based (testable without browser context)
Zero React imports
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/panic.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: PANIC background handler, message type, and Alt+P keyboard shortcut
user_stories.description: Two trigger paths: (1) Alt+P keyboard shortcut fires directly in background via browser.commands.onCommand — calls panic handler without going through message protocol. (2) PANIC message type for manual trigger from settings UI via browser.runtime.sendMessage. Both paths: check Pro status → check unlocked session → collectOpenTabs → buildPanicFolder → append to tree → save → open new blank tab → close all original tabs → optional history clear. Register Alt+P via chrome.commands in wxt.config.ts.
steps: Add PanicMessage { type: 'PANIC'; clearHistory?: boolean } to background-types.ts.
Add 'panic-button' command to wxt.config.ts manifest.commands: { 'panic-button': { 'suggested_key': { 'default': 'Alt+P' }, 'description': 'Panic: close all tabs and save URLs' } }.
Wire browser.commands.onCommand listener in defineBackground() for 'panic-button' → calls panic handler directly (no message protocol).
Add 'PANIC' to VALID_TYPES set and handleMessage switch in entrypoints/background/index.ts.
RED: Test: 'PANIC handler requires Pro status'
RED: Test: 'PANIC handler requires unlocked session'
RED: Test: 'PANIC handler collects tabs, saves panic folder, closes tabs'
RED: Test: 'PANIC handler opens new blank tab before closing original tabs'
RED: Test: 'PANIC handler clears history when clearHistory is true'
RED: Test: 'PANIC handler does not clear history when clearHistory is false/undefined'
RED: Test: 'PANIC handler does not close tabs if save fails'

GREEN: Implement handler. collectOpenTabs → buildPanicFolder → append folder to tree root → save → browser.tabs.create({}) (new blank tab) → browser.tabs.remove(originalTabIds) → optional clearBrowsingHistory.
Wire onCommand listener: read clearHistory preference from browser.storage.local, then call panic handler.
acceptance_criteria: PanicMessage type added to BackgroundMessage union in background-types.ts
'PANIC' added to VALID_TYPES set in background/index.ts
handleMessage switch has 'PANIC' case in background/index.ts
'panic-button' command registered in manifest via wxt.config.ts (not _execute_panic — underscore prefix is reserved)
Alt+P triggers via browser.commands.onCommand in defineBackground() — direct handler call, no message protocol
PANIC message type used only for manual trigger from settings UI
Handler checks Pro status — returns PRO_REQUIRED if not Pro
Handler requires unlocked session
Handler: collect tabs → build panic folder → save to tree → open new blank tab → close original tabs
Handler opens browser.tabs.create({}) BEFORE closing tabs (prevents all windows closing)
Tabs closed AFTER save succeeds (no data loss on save failure)
Handler: optional history clearing (only when clearHistory: true)
onCommand listener reads clearHistory preference from browser.storage.local
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/entrypoints/background.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit && npx wxt build

user_stories.title: Panic button settings UI, preference persistence, and E2E verification
user_stories.description: Add Panic Button configuration section in SettingsScreen (Pro-gated). Shows keyboard shortcut (Alt+P), history clearing toggle with runtime permission request, manual trigger button. Persist clearHistory preference to browser.storage.local. E2E tests for panic flow. Full verification.
steps: Create components/settings/PanicButtonConfig.tsx: displays Alt+P shortcut info, checkbox for 'Also clear browsing history', 'Trigger Panic' button for manual activation. Gated by useProGate().
History toggle: on enable, call browser.permissions.request({ permissions: ['history'] }). If denied, show info message and keep toggle unchecked.
Persist clearHistory preference to browser.storage.local key 'panicClearHistory' (boolean). Load on component mount.
Wire into SettingsScreen in Privacy section.
RED: Test: 'PanicButtonConfig shows shortcut info'
RED: Test: 'Free user sees UpgradePrompt instead of panic config'
RED: Test: 'History toggle requests permission when enabled'
RED: Test: 'History toggle reverts when permission denied'
RED: Test: 'Trigger Panic button sends PANIC message with clearHistory preference'
RED: Test: 'clearHistory preference persisted to storage.local'
RED: E2E: 'Panic button closes tabs and saves URLs'
RED: E2E: 'Panic save folder appears in tree after recovery'
GREEN: Implement component and E2E tests.
Run full verification
acceptance_criteria: PanicButtonConfig: Pro-gated (UpgradePrompt for free users)
Shows Alt+P shortcut info text
History clearing toggle (checkbox) with runtime permission request
Permission denial handled gracefully — toggle reverts, info message shown
clearHistory preference persisted to browser.storage.local key 'panicClearHistory'
Manual trigger button sends PANIC message with current clearHistory preference
E2E: tabs closed, panic folder created in tree, new blank tab opened
Full verification passes
Zero regressions
user_stories.verification: npx tsc --noEmit && npx eslint . && npx vitest run && npx wxt build && npm run test:e2e

what_exists.note: Read actual state from most recent progress.md. Requires Module 16a (ProGate) to be complete.
what_exists.commands: wxt.config.ts has _execute_action for Ctrl+Shift+H (from Module 9). commands.onCommand listener does NOT exist yet — must be added.
what_exists.types: lib/types.ts has Bookmark and Folder types — reuse these, do not create new ones

what_does_not_exist.panic: No lib/panic.ts. No PANIC message type. No Alt+P shortcut. No PanicButtonConfig component. No browser.commands.onCommand listener in background.

tech_stack.tabs_api: browser.tabs.query({}) for all tabs, browser.tabs.remove(tabIds) to close
tech_stack.history_api: browser.history.deleteAll() — requires 'history' permission (in optional_permissions). Must check browser.permissions.contains() before use and request via browser.permissions.request() in UI.
tech_stack.commands_api: chrome.commands via wxt.config.ts manifest.commands. Command name: 'panic-button' (NOT _execute_panic — underscore prefix reserved for browser commands). Background onCommand listener.
tech_stack.types: Reuse Bookmark and Folder from lib/types.ts. buildPanicFolder returns Folder with Bookmark children.
forbidden_patterns: Closing tabs BEFORE save succeeds (data loss risk)
Closing all tabs without opening a new blank tab first (leaves user with no windows)
Using _execute_* prefix for custom commands (reserved by Chrome)
Creating new bookmark/folder types instead of reusing lib/types.ts

panic_philosophy.data_safety: SAVE before CLOSE. Tabs are only closed after panic folder is successfully saved to the encrypted tree. If save fails, tabs stay open and user gets an error. Always open a new blank tab before closing originals.
panic_philosophy.privacy_first: History clearing is OPT-IN. Default: false. User must explicitly enable it in settings. Permission requested at runtime.
panic_philosophy.stays_client_side: Panic button is a pure client-side action. No sync, no backend dependency. A forker gets the full feature.

read_first: lib/types.ts (Bookmark and Folder types to reuse)

outputs.description: Tab collection + panic folder builder + history clearing
Panic button configuration UI with permission management

known_risks.issue: Alt+P may conflict with other extensions or OS shortcuts
known_risks.action: Chrome allows user to customize via chrome://extensions/shortcuts. Alt+P is suggested, not enforced. Document in settings UI.

known_risks.issue: History permission may not be granted
known_risks.action: Request at runtime via browser.permissions.request(). If denied, revert toggle and show info message. clearBrowsingHistory returns false gracefully.
await save THEN open new blank tab THEN close original tabs. Do not close before save confirms.

known_risks.issue: Closing all tabs closes all windows
known_risks.action: Open a new blank tab via browser.tabs.create({}) BEFORE closing originals. User always has at least one window.

dependency_chain.parallel_safe: Can run in parallel with 16c and 16e

format_rules: Status: PASSED (attempt N) or FAILED (attempt N)

metadata.quality_gate: All tests pass. tsc clean. panic-button command in manifest. Save before close. New tab before close. Pro gated. History permission guarded.
metadata.human_review_required: Verify: tabs closed AFTER save, new blank tab opened before close, Alt+P shortcut works, history clearing opt-in only with runtime permission.

execution_rules.prd_scope: Module 16d of 22. Pro feature. Client-side only.
